materialization_policy.core: Non-conditional task deliverables from aiPromptHint; actionableQuestion is fallback only.
materialization_policy.supporting: Conditional deliverables such as when available or if a stated condition applies.
materialization_policy.constraints: Causal, scope, missing-data, audience, and non-invention restrictions are recorded separately.
materialization_policy.review_overrides: Task-specific education-dashboard review overrides may refine core/supporting/constraint classification without deleting source provenance.
materialization_policy.safety_fairness: Conservative default applicable; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.
materialization_policy.freeze_rule: Runner must copy the reviewed requirements into each frozen judge input.

source.actionable_question: How am I performing overall?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Summarise overall score, pass/fail status, class benchmark, percentile, and the most useful next action based only on returned fields.

required_core_outputs.description: State the overall score and pass/fail status.

required_supporting_outputs.description: Compare against the class benchmark when class benchmark fields are present.
Report percentile standing when score_percentile is present.
Suggest the most useful next action supported by returned fields.

evaluation_constraints.description: Use only returned fields; do not fill missing benchmark or percentile values with invented estimates.
If percentile data is absent, omit percentile comparison and state that it is unavailable when relevant.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Am I at risk of failing?
source.ai_prompt_hint: State the risk badge (low/medium/high) and at_risk_score out of 5. Use avg_score, pass_threshold, engagement_score, and punctuality_rate when available. If risk is high, name the top 2 likely triggered factors from returned metrics only.

required_core_outputs.description: State the risk badge (low/medium/high) and at_risk_score out of 5.

required_supporting_outputs.description: Use avg_score, pass_threshold, engagement_score, and punctuality_rate when available.
If risk is high, name the top 2 likely triggered factors from returned metrics only.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: How active am I compared to classmates?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Describe effort level in plain language using study_effort_level, total_engagement_count, active_days, engagement_score, and class_avg_engagement_score. Compare to class averages only from returned fields, and avoid calling missing engagement data low effort.

required_core_outputs.description: Characterise the student's effort level relative to the available class benchmark in plain language.

evaluation_constraints.description: Ground the characterisation in study_effort_level, total_engagement_count, active_days, engagement_score, and class_avg_engagement_score when those fields are returned.
Do not label missing engagement data as low effort.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Am I getting better or worse over time?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Identify trend direction, assessments below pass/target thresholds, and the concrete recommended_action for the weakest recent assessment.

required_core_outputs.description: Identify the observed score trend direction.
Identify assessments below returned pass or target thresholds.

required_supporting_outputs.description: Provide recommended_action for the weakest recent assessment only when that field is present.

evaluation_constraints.description: If fewer than 3 assessment data points are available, state that evidence is insufficient for a reliable trend rather than asserting a stable direction.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Which skill area should I prioritise for improvement?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Highlight which competency tags have lowest avg scores. Suggest focus areas.

required_core_outputs.description: Identify the competency tags with the lowest average scores.

required_supporting_outputs.description: Suggest focus areas grounded in the identified competency gaps.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Where do I stand compared to my class?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Show student's standing (top X%). Explain which metrics are above/below average.

required_core_outputs.description: Show student's standing (top X%).
Explain which metrics are above/below average.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: What specific risk signals are active for me right now?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Treat this as a checklist: list triggered flags first, then explain each using flag_value vs threshold, severity, flag_description, and recommended_action. Keep non-triggered flags brief.

required_core_outputs.description: List triggered risk flags first.
Explain each triggered flag using its value, threshold, severity, description, and recommended action when available.

evaluation_constraints.description: If no flags are triggered, state that explicitly.
Keep non-triggered flags brief.
Do not invent risk signals that are not present in returned flags.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Which weeks did I disengage and why might that be?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Describe engagement pattern. Flag weeks with sharp drops. Note if drop precedes assessment.

required_core_outputs.description: Describe engagement pattern.
Flag weeks with sharp drops.
Note if drop precedes assessment.

evaluation_constraints.description: Treat temporal proximity between engagement drops and assessments as an association, not proof that the assessment caused the drop.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Am I studying steadily or only before exams?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Distinguish 'steady learner' from 'pre-exam crammer'. Recommend a consistent weekly routine.

required_core_outputs.description: Characterise the observed study pattern as steady or concentrated before assessments when evidence supports that distinction.

required_supporting_outputs.description: Recommend a consistent weekly routine grounded in the observed study pattern.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: How much are my absences hurting my grades?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Use absence_rate [FE] to show proportion of missed sessions. Correlate with avg_score [FE].

required_core_outputs.description: State the proportion of missed sessions.
Describe the observed association between absence rate and average score.

evaluation_constraints.description: Use absence_rate as the primary absence metric.
Frame the absence-score relationship as correlational, not causal.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Does submitting late actually lower my score?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Use submission_delay_avg [FE] and punctuality_rate [FE]. Explain how delay magnitude correlates with score drop.

required_core_outputs.description: State average submission delay and punctuality rate.
Describe the observed relationship between delay magnitude and score.

evaluation_constraints.description: Use submission_delay_avg and punctuality_rate when returned.
Frame the delay-score relationship as correlational, not causal.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Could my lifestyle habits be affecting my academic results?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Compare the selected student against the class lifestyle-risk scatter. Highlight the student's position and any cohort-level association between lifestyle_risk_score and avg_score. Frame as correlation, not causation.

required_core_outputs.description: Compare the selected student against the class lifestyle-risk scatter.
Highlight the student's position and any cohort-level association between lifestyle_risk_score and avg_score.

evaluation_constraints.description: Frame as correlation, not causation.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Am I using the full range of learning resources available?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Use vle_diversity_score [FE] to show breadth. Highlight under-used types (e.g. forum rarely used).

required_core_outputs.description: State the student's resource usage breadth across available VLE types.
Identify under-used resource types when a comparison baseline is available.

evaluation_constraints.description: Use vle_diversity_score as the primary resource-breadth metric when returned.
Do not label low usage of a resource type as disengagement when no comparison baseline is provided.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Did enrolling late put me at a disadvantage?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Explain whether early enrollment correlates with better outcome. Frame as associative, not causal.

required_core_outputs.description: Explain whether early enrollment correlates with better outcome.

evaluation_constraints.description: Frame as associative, not causal.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Am I a procrastinator and is it costing me marks?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Use submission_delay_avg [FE]. Identify if late submission is systematic. Link to score.

required_core_outputs.description: Identify whether late submission is systematic.
Describe the observed relationship between submission delay and score.

evaluation_constraints.description: Use submission_delay_avg as the primary delay metric; do not infer procrastination from score alone.
Frame the delay-score relationship as correlational, not causal.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: What should I do differently starting next week?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Synthesise all [FE] risk signals into 3–5 prioritised actions. Reference which FE feature triggered each item.

required_core_outputs.description: Synthesise all [FE] risk signals into 3–5 prioritised actions.
Reference which FE feature triggered each item.

evaluation_constraints.description: Do not include actions that reference signals not present in returned data.
Do not invent risk context, urgency, or priority unsupported by returned feature-engineered signals.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Is my social life balanced with my academic commitments?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Compare the selected student against the class social-balance scatter. Highlight the student's position and any cohort-level association between social_balance_score and avg_score. Frame as correlation, not causation.

required_core_outputs.description: Compare the selected student against the class social-balance scatter.
Highlight the student's position and any cohort-level association between social_balance_score and avg_score.

evaluation_constraints.description: Frame as correlation, not causation.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: How might my family background be reflected in my academic patterns?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Compare the selected student against the class family-context scatter. Highlight the student's position and any cohort-level association between family_stability_score and avg_score. Frame findings as associative, not causal, and avoid prescriptive suggestions.

required_core_outputs.description: Compare the selected student against the class family-context scatter.
Highlight the student's position and any cohort-level association between family_stability_score and avg_score.

evaluation_constraints.description: Frame findings as associative, not causal, and avoid prescriptive suggestions.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: How is the class performing overall?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Describe score spread. Flag if large proportion below pass threshold.

required_core_outputs.description: Describe score spread.
Flag if large proportion below pass threshold.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: How many students passed, failed, or withdrew?
source.ai_prompt_hint: State pass/fail/withdrawal counts. Highlight proportion at-risk.

required_core_outputs.description: State pass/fail/withdrawal counts.
Highlight proportion at-risk.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: What proportion of the class is disengaged?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Describe proportion of low/medium/high effort students. Flag the low tail.

required_core_outputs.description: Describe proportion of low/medium/high effort students.
Flag the low tail.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: How many students need urgent intervention?
source.ai_prompt_hint: State number of high/medium/low risk students. Recommend immediate review of high group.

required_core_outputs.description: State number of high/medium/low risk students.
Recommend immediate review of high group.

source.actionable_question: Who is this student and what is their current overall situation?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Summarise who this student is and where they stand across all dimensions (score, engagement, risk) in plain language.

required_core_outputs.description: Summarise who this student is and where they stand across all dimensions (score, engagement, risk) in plain language.

evaluation_constraints.description: Do not extrapolate beyond returned score, engagement, and risk dimensions.
Avoid holistic judgements about the student when supporting data is absent.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Is this student getting better or worse — and how fast?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Identify whether student is improving or declining. Reference performance_trend [FE] slope direction and magnitude.

required_core_outputs.description: Identify whether student is improving or declining.
Reference performance_trend [FE] slope direction and magnitude.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: When exactly did this student start disengaging?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Flag the exact week engagement dropped. Compare to pre-drop average. Recommend admin outreach timing.

required_core_outputs.description: Identify the specific week in which engagement dropped when the data supports one.
Compare engagement after the drop with the pre-drop average.

required_supporting_outputs.description: When a specific drop week is identified, recommend outreach timing relative to that week.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Which specific risk factors should admin address for this student?
source.ai_prompt_hint: For each triggered flag, state the exact value and why it crosses the threshold. Prioritise the top 2 for immediate action.

required_core_outputs.description: For each triggered flag, state the exact value and why it crosses the threshold.
Prioritise the top 2 for immediate action.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Where should we focus academic support for this student?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Highlight competency tags with lowest avg scores. Recommend specific support type (knowledge_recall → flashcards, applied → practice problems).

required_core_outputs.description: Highlight competency tags with lowest avg scores.
Recommend specific support type (knowledge_recall → flashcards, applied → practice problems).

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Is this student consistently submitting late and does it affect their score?
source.ai_prompt_hint: State average delay and punctuality rate. Identify if late submission is systematic. Suggest deadline reminder or check-in.

required_core_outputs.description: State average delay and punctuality rate.
Identify if late submission is systematic.
Suggest deadline reminder or check-in.

evaluation_constraints.description: Do not characterise late submission as low motivation or a personal failing.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: What background factors should admin consider when deciding how to support this student?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Frame background factors as context, not judgement. Note which disadvantage dimensions are present and what support is already in place.

required_core_outputs.description: Note which disadvantage dimensions are present and what support is already in place.

evaluation_constraints.description: Frame background factors as context, not judgement.
Treat the output as professional advisor/admin context; do not expose raw disadvantage scores in student-facing wording.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: What should admin do for this student in the next 7 days?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Synthesise all [FE] signals into 3–5 admin actions ranked by urgency. Specify who should act (tutor / admin / counsellor) and by when.

required_core_outputs.description: Synthesise all [FE] signals into 3–5 admin actions ranked by urgency.
Specify who should act (tutor / admin / counsellor) and by when.

evaluation_constraints.description: Every proposed action and urgency level must reference returned feature-engineered signals.
Do not invent urgency that is not supported by returned signals.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Which student is improving faster and when did their paths diverge?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Explain divergence points. Identify when one student fell behind relative to the other.

required_core_outputs.description: Explain divergence points.
Identify when one student fell behind relative to the other.

evaluation_constraints.description: If either student's data is absent or insufficient for trajectory comparison, state that explicitly rather than inferring from the other student's data.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Which student is more engaged across total clicks, active days, and combined engagement score?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Compare the two students using metric, engagement_score, total_clicks, and active_days. Explain which engagement dimension creates the largest gap. Do not infer academic risk unless risk fields are present.

required_core_outputs.description: Compare the two students using metric, engagement_score, total_clicks, and active_days.
Explain which engagement dimension creates the largest gap.

evaluation_constraints.description: Do not infer academic risk unless risk fields are present.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Who is at higher risk and what are the key differences?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Compare the two returned student rows. Use at_risk_score, at_risk_label, and explicit flag_* columns to explain who needs more urgent intervention. If avg_score is null, state that score evidence is missing rather than guessing.

required_core_outputs.description: Compare the two returned student rows.
Use at_risk_score, at_risk_label, and explicit flag_* columns to explain who needs more urgent intervention.

required_supporting_outputs.description: If avg_score is null, state that score evidence is missing rather than guessing.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Which lifestyle context dimensions differ most between these two students?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Compare lifestyle_dimension rows for the two students. Highlight the largest dimension gaps. Use composite_lifestyle_risk_score and social_balance_score only as supporting context. Frame lifestyle differences as context only, not causal judgement.

required_core_outputs.description: Compare lifestyle_dimension rows for the two students.
Highlight the largest dimension gaps.

required_supporting_outputs.description: Use composite_lifestyle_risk_score and social_balance_score only as supporting context.

evaluation_constraints.description: Frame lifestyle differences as context only, not causal judgement.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Applicable because this is an individual-level comparison involving lifestyle context.

source.actionable_question: Does one student face more structural disadvantage than the other?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Identify background-driven performance differences. Avoid causal claims.

required_core_outputs.description: Identify background-driven performance differences.

evaluation_constraints.description: Avoid causal claims.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Is one student using resources more strategically than the other?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Highlight who is more diversified in resource use. Note if one avoids forums/quizzes.

required_core_outputs.description: Highlight who is more diversified in resource use.
Note if one avoids forums/quizzes.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Retained as applicable because it compares two identifiable students; risk is lower than lifestyle/background tasks but not absent.

source.actionable_question: Which students are dangerously disengaged and need outreach?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Define 'low engagement' threshold. List students below it. Recommend admin action (email/contact).

required_core_outputs.description: Define 'low engagement' threshold.
List students below it.
Recommend admin action (email/contact).

evaluation_constraints.description: Do not name individual students beyond identifiers already present in returned data.
Treat the output as internal admin use when individual identifiers are listed.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Does engaging more in this class actually lead to better grades?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Describe correlation direction and strength. Flag outliers (high engagement, low score) as a special case.

required_core_outputs.description: Describe correlation direction and strength.
Flag outliers (high engagement, low score) as a special case.

evaluation_constraints.description: Describe the engagement-score relationship as correlational; do not infer that engagement causes score outcomes.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Who should the admin contact first this week?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Rank at-risk students by at_risk_score. For each high-risk student, explain triggered_flags_summary and recommend the recommended_admin_action. Do not invent reasons outside triggered_flags.

required_core_outputs.description: Rank at-risk students by at_risk_score.
For each high-risk student, explain triggered_flags_summary and recommend the recommended_admin_action.

evaluation_constraints.description: Do not invent reasons outside triggered_flags.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Which assessment is causing the most students to fail?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Flag assessments with pass_flag=False rate above threshold. Suggest curriculum review.

required_core_outputs.description: Flag assessments with pass_flag=False rate above threshold.
Suggest curriculum review.

evaluation_constraints.description: Frame a high fail rate as a signal for assessment or curriculum review, not as evidence of student deficiency.
Avoid causal claims about student quality.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Retained as applicable because curriculum recommendations can affect learners; candidate for human review rather than automatic exclusion.

source.actionable_question: Are late submissions a systemic problem in this class?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Use final_outcome, assessment_type, submission_delay_avg, late_submission_rate, and punctuality_rate to decide whether late submission is systemic. Prioritize groups with high late_submission_rate and high student_count. Do not discuss individual students.

required_core_outputs.description: Use final_outcome, assessment_type, submission_delay_avg, late_submission_rate, and punctuality_rate to decide whether late submission is systemic.
Prioritize groups with high late_submission_rate and high student_count.

evaluation_constraints.description: Do not discuss individual students.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Which learning activities should the admin encourage students to use more?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Highlight which resource types are most associated with higher scores. Recommend admin to promote those.

required_core_outputs.description: Highlight which resource types are most associated with higher scores.
Recommend admin to promote those.

evaluation_constraints.description: Frame resource-score relationships as correlational; do not claim that a resource type causes score improvement.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: What are the strongest predictors of student success in this dataset?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Rank top 5 features by correlation with avg_score [FE]. Note dataset-specific features.

required_core_outputs.description: Rank top 5 features by correlation with avg_score [FE].
Note dataset-specific features.

evaluation_constraints.description: When ranked features include sensitive demographic, lifestyle, or socioeconomic attributes, identify their sensitivity and avoid framing correlation as prescriptive guidance.
Do not interpret feature correlation as causal importance.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Retained as applicable pending review because ranked factors may include sensitive attributes and may influence intervention policy.

source.actionable_question: Which demographic groups are scoring or engaging below the class average?
source.ai_prompt_hint: For each demographic group, compare mean avg_score [FE] and engagement_score [FE] to cohort average. Use colour intensity to show deviation. Avoid causal claims. Note: output is continuous score — distinct from A-G12 which uses categorical final_outcome.

required_core_outputs.description: For each demographic group, compare mean average score and engagement score with the cohort average.

evaluation_constraints.description: Quantify the magnitude of group deviation rather than relying on visual colour encoding alone.
Avoid causal claims.
Treat this as an equity-sensitive demographic group analysis.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Applicable because the task compares demographic groups and requires equity framing.

source.actionable_question: Are disadvantaged students receiving adequate support?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Describe trend. Highlight which imd_band groups have most at-risk students. Recommend equity-aware support.

required_core_outputs.description: Describe trend.
Highlight which imd_band groups have most at-risk students.
Recommend equity-aware support.

evaluation_constraints.description: Describe group-level patterns only; do not name individual students in equity analysis.
Frame socioeconomic factors as context and association, not individual blame or deterministic cause.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Applicable because the task analyses socioeconomic disadvantage and recommends equity-aware support.

source.actionable_question: How many students are cramming instead of studying consistently?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Describe the distribution of students across high, medium, and low consistency using student_count and pct_students. Use avg_weekly_stddev and avg_active_weeks to explain what low consistency means, then recommend study-routine interventions for the low group.

required_core_outputs.description: Describe the distribution of students across high, medium, and low consistency using returned cohort counts or percentages.
Explain what low consistency means by reference to returned spread and active-week metrics.
Recommend study-routine interventions targeted at the low-consistency group.

evaluation_constraints.description: Do not conflate low consistency with low effort.
Acknowledge that observed study patterns may reflect employment, health, accessibility, or family obligations when making recommendations.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Which weeks should the admin schedule check-ins or motivational nudges?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Use early_warning_week [FE] to pinpoint critical weeks. Recommend admin action timing (e.g. week 5 intervention).

required_core_outputs.description: Identify the critical weeks in which cohort-level engagement declined.
Recommend admin action timing relative to the identified critical weeks.

evaluation_constraints.description: Use early_warning_week as the primary timing field when returned.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Which demographic groups have the highest failure or dropout rate?
source.ai_prompt_hint: For each demographic group, state fail rate and withdrawal rate as percentages. Flag groups where fail+withdrawn > cohort threshold. Note: output is categorical final_outcome — distinct from A-G08 which uses continuous avg_score.

required_core_outputs.description: For each demographic group, state fail rate and withdrawal rate as percentages.
Flag groups where fail+withdrawn > cohort threshold.

evaluation_constraints.description: Do not conflate categorical pass, fail, or withdrawal outcomes with continuous score metrics when interpreting group results.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Applicable because the task compares failure and withdrawal rates across demographic groups.
tasks.review_note: A-G12 evaluates categorical final outcomes; A-G08 evaluates continuous score and engagement metrics.

source.actionable_question: Which lifestyle patterns are most common among low-performing students?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Rank students by lifestyle_risk_score. Correlate with avg_score. Frame as correlational, not prescriptive.

required_core_outputs.description: Rank students by lifestyle_risk_score.
Correlate with avg_score.

evaluation_constraints.description: Frame lifestyle-risk ranking as descriptive and correlational, not causal or deterministic.
When listing students by lifestyle-risk rank, avoid language that implies individual blame.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Applicable because the task ranks identifiable students using lifestyle context.

source.actionable_question: How early can admin detect a student about to drop out?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Use early_warning_week [FE] to show when engagement collapsed for withdrawn students. Compare to passing students.

required_core_outputs.description: Identify when engagement collapsed for withdrawn students.
Compare the timing or trajectory with passing students.

evaluation_constraints.description: Use early_warning_week as the primary collapse-timing field when returned.
Frame the comparison as an observed pattern, not proof of withdrawal causation.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: Who are the top 10 students most in need of intervention right now?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Rank by at_risk_score [FE] descending. List triggered flags per student. Provide action per priority group.

required_core_outputs.description: Rank by at_risk_score [FE] descending.
List triggered flags per student.
Provide action per priority group.

evaluation_constraints.description: Treat the output as internal admin use only.
Do not include personally identifying information beyond identifiers already present in returned data.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.

source.actionable_question: What concrete actions should the admin take in the next 2 weeks?
source.ai_prompt_hint: Synthesise all cohort [FE] signals into 3–5 admin actions. This is the most critical AI synthesis task for admin role.

required_core_outputs.description: Synthesise cohort feature-engineered signals into 3–5 admin actions.

evaluation_constraints.description: Every recommended action must be grounded in returned cohort feature-engineered signals.
Do not invent urgency or priority without supporting returned data.

tasks.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.
tasks.review_note: Removed descriptive task-importance metadata from core deliverables during human review.
